qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL Injection via unsanitized GET parameter righe in confronta_righe.php modal
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SQL injection in confronta_righe.php via the righe parameter, which is a different file and parameter than the advisory's ajax_complete.php get_sedi endpoint with idanagrafica parameter. Different vulnerable code location, different source-to-sink path.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal / arbitrary file read via unsanitized filename parameter in importFE/view.php
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a path traversal vulnerability in importFE/view.php, which is a different vulnerability class (CWE-22) than the advisory's SQL injection (CWE-89), and a different file location entirely.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described SQL injection vulnerability in modules/anagrafiche/ajax/complete.php get_sedi endpoint via the idanagrafica parameter. The two qualifying findings describe: (1) SQL injection in a different file (confronta_righe.php) with a different parameter (righe), and (2) path traversal in a different file (importFE/view.php). Neither matches the vulnerable code location or root cause described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory specifies vulnerable file: modules/anagrafiche/ajax/complete.php line 28 in get_sedi case
Advisory specifies vulnerable parameter: idanagrafica via $_GET['idanagrafica']
Finding sqli-via-get-righe targets confronta_righe.php with righe parameter - different file and parameter
Finding path-traversal-importfe-view targets importFE/view.php - different vulnerability class and file
